Solution of Cd(C2H2-HOH) + CH3-COOH and Cd-O + H2SO4
Pile oscillator method using boron as a monitor
The neutron flux response to the oscillation
was detected by ion chambers in the reflector
Westcott formalism to reduce to 2200 m/sec. values.
The value is a weighted average of 3 measurements
The values deduced on the basis of Westcott's 'g' and
's' factors are generally in agreement with other
the standard deviations as well as the
uncertainties in the concentration of about 5%

Solution of Gd(2)-O(3) + H2SO4 in D2O
Pile oscillator method using boron as a monitor
The neutron flux response to the oscillation
was detected by ion chambers in the reflector
Westcott formalism to reduce to 2200 m/sec. values.
The value is a weighted average of 4 measurements
The chopper measured value of the absorption cross
section is higher, confirming earlier findings and
indicating that the Westcott g factor for gadolinium
the standard deviations as well as the
uncertainties in the concentration of about 5%
The value agrees with the value 48660+-400 barns
obtained with a crystal spectrometer (see REL-REF).
These values are systematically higher than those
obtained in integral measurements, indicating that
the Westcott 'g' factor for gadolinium is too high

Solution of Lu2(SO4)3 + H2SO4 in D2O
Pile oscillator method using boron as a monitor
The neutron flux response to the oscillation
was detected by ion chambers in the reflector
Westcott formalism to reduce to 2200 m/sec. values.
The value is the weighted average of 4 measurements.
There is a large discrepancy in the cross section
values obtained by the integral technique and those
obtained from differential measurements.  The dis-
crepancy is so large that the assumptions about the
cross section shape (1/V dependence) seem to be
